key_insight: A physics-informed boundary-masked attention mechanism improves out-of-distribution grain growth prediction by focusing the model on grain boundary pixels.
engineering_relevance: Deep learning models trained on synthetic microstructures can generalize to real and atypical grain growth cases, with physics-guided attention offering a practical accuracy boost.
method: Tested a previously trained grain-growth model on three OOD cases: experimental microstructures, bimodal grain size distributions, and abnormal grain growth.
Compared a baseline model with a boundary-masked attention variant that restricts attention to grain boundary pixels.
Evaluated both models without retraining or fine-tuning using SSIM and mean grain size error.
evidence_signal: The strongest gain occurred for bimodal microstructures, where SSIM improved from 0.6221 to 0.7609 and mean grain size error dropped from 8.75% to 3.57%.
watch_item: The improvement depends on boundary morphology matching the training domain, so benefits may be weaker for microstructures that deviate strongly from learned grain-boundary patterns.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Attention Mechanism and Generalization Capability of Deep Learning-Based Grain Growth Evolution Prediction
